definition: Feeling troubled and nervous, with some emotional unrest
example: The long wait made her visibly agitated.
The fire alarm started ringing, and everyone walked out of the building.

definition: A warning signal or message that tells people to prepare for danger.
example: The government sent a text alert to everyone warning about the incoming storm.

definition: A vehicle equipped for taking sick or injured people to the hospital.
example: The ambulance rushed the patient to the emergency room with sirens wailing.
He felt a surge of anger when he was unfairly blamed for the mistake.
She felt annoyance when people interrupted her while she was speaking.
Years of bitterness had left her unable to trust anyone.

definition: A person who is present at an event or incident but does not take part in it.
example: A brave bystander jumped into the river to pull the struggling child to safety.

definition: A person killed or injured in a war or accident.
example: Fortunately, there were no casualties in the building collapse because everyone escaped.

definition: An organization that organizes help and safety measures for citizens during a war or natural disaster.
example: Civil defense volunteers distributed blankets and food to the storm victims.

definition: A line of police, soldiers, or guards preventing access to or from an area.
example: Police established a cordon around the gas leak to keep the public at a safe distance.

definition: A rule requiring people to remain indoors between specified hours, typically at night.
example: The mayor announced a curfew starting at 8 PM to keep residents safe during the riots.

definition: A person who receives emergency calls and sends help where it is needed.
example: The emergency dispatcher asked for my address and told me to stay calm.

definition: A signal from a ship, aircraft, or person indicating they are in serious danger and need help.
example: The sinking ship fired a red flare into the sky as a distress signal.
The school holds a fire drill every semester so students know how to exit safely.

definition: A special telephone number used to reach emergency services like police, fire, or ambulance.
example: Make sure children know the emergency number for your country.

definition: The public organizations that deal with emergencies, mainly the police, fire, and ambulance services.
example: Dial 911 to contact emergency services in North America.

definition: Very angry; furious.
example: He was enraged by the blatant disrespect shown to his family.

meaning: खाली करना / सुरक्षित स्थान पर ले जाना
definition: To leave a dangerous place, or to make people leave a dangerous place.
example: The police helped residents evacuate the low-lying areas before the river flooded.

definition: Intensely irritated and frustrated.
example: He was exasperated after explaining the same rule for the tenth time.

definition: Someone whose job is to go immediately to help people at the scene of an emergency, such as a police officer or paramedic.
example: First responders arrived at the crash site within five minutes to rescue the drivers.

definition: A device that produces a bright light or flame, used to signal for help or warn drivers in the dark.
example: The police placed orange flares on the road to warn drivers of the accident ahead.
He felt frustration when the software kept crashing during his presentation.

definition: Bad-tempered and sulky.
example: He was grumpy without his morning coffee.
The hostility in his tone made it clear he was not willing to cooperate.

definition: A direct telephone line set up for rapid communication in emergencies.
example: You can call the emergency hotline if you witness a safety hazard on the highway.
Her impatience grew with every minute the bus was delayed.

definition: Extremely angered; filled with burning rage
example: The crowd was incensed by the unfair verdict.

definition: Feeling or characterized by great anger.
example: The irate customer demanded to speak to the manager.

definition: A tendency to be easily annoyed or made angry
example: Lack of sleep increased his irritability and he snapped at minor things.

definition: Showing or feeling slight anger; annoyed.
example: He was irritated by the constant noise from the street.
The constant noise from outside caused her great irritation.
Jealousy crept in when he saw his colleague receiving all the praise.

definition: Furiously angry.
example: She was absolutely livid when she discovered the betrayal.

definition: A state of isolation or restricted access instituted as a security measure.
example: The school went into lockdown after a dangerous individual was spotted nearby.

definition: An international radio word used by pilots and sailors to signal life-threatening distress.
example: The pilot shouted 'Mayday, Mayday' into the radio when the engine failed.

definition: To gather together, especially for inspection or during an emergency.
example: The crew members had to muster on the deck within three minutes of the alarm.

definition: An international radio signal used to declare an urgent situation that is not yet life-threatening.
example: The captain used a pan-pan call to report that the ship had run out of fuel.
He was in a fit of rage after discovering someone had stolen from him.

definition: Feeling or expressing bitterness or indignation at having been treated unfairly.
example: He was resentful of the favoritism shown to his sibling.
She harboured deep resentment towards her boss for taking credit for her work.

definition: An area or portion that is distinct from others, often used by rescue operations.
example: Rescue teams divided the flooded city into sectors to search for survivors systematically.

definition: An instruction to stay inside your current building and take cover rather than trying to evacuate.
example: Residents were told to shelter-in-place during the chemical leak to avoid breathing the air outside.

definition: A device that makes a loud, prolonged sound as a warning signal.
example: We heard the fire engine's siren from a block away.

definition: An international distress signal used to call for help, especially by ships or in remote areas.
example: The lost hikers spelled out SOS with rocks on the ground so rescue planes could see it.

definition: Quietly resentful and moody, withdrawing to show displeasure
example: She became sulky when her request was turned down.

definition: A person who remains alive after a dangerous event or disaster.
example: Rescue teams searched the rubble for survivors of the earthquake.

definition: Strongly desiring revenge; intent on punishing those who wronged you
example: Her vengeful mood made it hard for others to approach her.
